Prettier Tutorial: You'll End Up Using It Anyway — Why Not Start Now? (Full VSCode Setup)

Prettier is one of those frontend tools you have to know. Even if you're not using it today, you'll end up using it eventually — so why not start now? This post walks through installing Prettier, configuring it, setting it up in VSCode, and how it differs from ESLint.
Maybe You Should Know a Bit About CI/CD: Deploying to Render.com with CD

Knowing a bit about CI/CD is genuinely useful as a developer — even on a side project, you can put it to work. This post covers what CI/CD actually is, the YAML basics behind GitHub Actions, how workflows are structured (Event → Runner → Job → Step), and a full walkthrough of building a PR-check workflow (ESLint + Jest) and a deploy workflow that ships to Render.com via Deploy Hooks.

This post walks through adding ESLint to a Vue Vite project, using the Airbnb or Standard style guide.
How to Switch ESLint Style (Standard → Airbnb) Without Losing Your Mind

When you build a project you'll almost always reach for a style manager to keep everyone's code consistent — on the frontend that's ESLint. The thing is, once you've picked a style at the start, switching to a different one later is a pain. So here's how to move ESLint from the Standard style over to Airbnb.

A walkthrough on how to make VSCode auto-fix ESLint warnings and errors, including how to handle formatting conflicts between ESLint and Prettier.

This post walks through how to add a JavaScript style manager to your own project. If you want your project's code to have a baseline level of quality, ESLint is basically a must-have. We'll install it, run through the init questions, and compare the Airbnb, Standard, and Google style guides.
